mechanism_narrative: Growth in total nonfarm payrolls signals expanding household income and aggregate demand, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 55% of months with rising PAYEMS.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.25%, the persistently low discount rate reduces the cost of capital for equities and suppresses short-term risk-free returns, driving capital into the S&P 500 and producing positive 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are negative in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, or the strategy underperforms buy-and-hold by more than 2% annualized over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in retail sales and a decline in the unemployment rate jointly signal synchronized demand and labor market strength, amplifying earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The two-variable interaction model produces lower walk-forward Rank IC than either RSAFS or UNRATE alone over a 2-year period, or 20-day forward returns are negative when both series move in the predicted direction.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns specifically when the unemployment rate exceeds 6%, because output recoveries in high-slack environments generate operating leverage before wage and input cost pressures emerge.
failure_condition: INDPRO fails to produce positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of months when UNRATE exceeds 6%, or the conditional Sharpe ratio in this regime is below 0.2 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8% signals excess labor market slack that depresses wage growth and consumer spending, leading to downward earnings revisions and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of months when UNRATE exceeds 8%, or the negative signal fails to achieve a t-statistic below -1.5 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month increase in the CPI level exceeding 2% signals accelerating inflation that erodes real incomes and raises discount rate expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative 5-day returns occur in less than 55% of triggered months, or walk-forward Rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in the federal funds rate and the consumer price level signal a monetary tightening into an inflationary environment, which jointly compresses corporate margins and equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising FEDFUNDS and rising CPIAUCSL fails to produce negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences, or the interaction term is statistically insignificant with p-value greater than 0.05 in a 3-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP signals expanding aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in less than 55% of quarters with positive GDPC1 growth, or walk-forward Rank IC is below 0.03 over a 3-year window.
